title: hieracium scapigerum subsp. bellii
intro: Hieracium scapigerum subsp. bellii, also known as the hawkweed or Hieracium bellii, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Hieracium bellii include its potential to treat urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Hieracium bellii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can also be used in salads or as a garnish, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
